issues.title: Support Bedrock to set timeout
issues.body:

Bedrock cannot set timeout. I am willing to contribute this function and add timeout setting.

comments.body: This doesn't seem to be working and still times out fast when making calls through bedrock
I am also still seeing this issue
definitely still seeing this issue. When using the BedrockAnthropic3ChatModel it times out at 2 minutes

issues.title: ERNIE Bot LLM supporting Qianfan

comments.body:
There is now support for [QianFan](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/qianfan-chat.html)  does this meet your needs?  

@mxsl-gr What are your thoughts?

@markpollack Sorry, I've been busy with other projects lately. I didn't notice this issue when implementing the QianFan model, i think can be closed.
﻿
Hi, @fanjia1024 the version `1.0.0-M2` has not been released yet. I suggest you pull the `main` branch code, run `mvn install` or `mvn deploy` it to your private repo, and use version `1.0.0-SNAPSHOT`.
﻿
for QianFan's specific configurations, you can refer to the documentation at [`/spring-ai-docs/src/main/antora/modules/ROOT/pages/api/chat/qianfan-chat.adoc`](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/spring-ai-docs/src/main/antora/modules/ROOT/pages/api/chat/qianfan-chat.adoc#sample-controller)

issues.title: The SDK of the vector database is very inflexible, unable to modify some database fields.

comments.body: The cost of rebuilding a vector database that has already been put into production in order to use Spring AI is enormous.

I faced a similar issue with Azure AI Search, where I had imported some data into the vector store using Azure's inbuilt solution for importing and chunking data into Azure AI Search. The import process resulted in different field names compared to the vector store implementations in Spring AI. And when I was trying to do a vector search using Spring AI, it failed. 
The only way that I could think of resolving the issue was to create your own implementation of the Vector Store and model the fields in the code with the same name as in Azure AI Search.

Thanks for reporting this, sorry for the delay.   @iAMSagar44 wrt to the comment "The import process resulted in different field names compared to the vector store implementations in Spring AI."   Were the field names different or were the different fields.  I agree, we need to open up the names that are used.  A simple way would be to create setters for all the current field names, what do you think?

> Thanks for reporting this, sorry for the delay. [@iAMSagar44](https://github.com/iAMSagar44) wrt to the comment "The import process resulted in different field names compared to the vector store implementations in Spring AI." Were the field names different or were the different fields. I agree, we need to open up the names that are used. A simple way would be to create setters for all the current field names, what do you think?

@markpollack, This issue persists in the AzureVectorStore and does not appear to have been resolved in the 1.0.0 release.
